page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU encryption testing placing in the top 25%. Memory subsystem performance is highly efficient, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 10%, despite uncached large-block memory read bandwidth averaging 128.39 GB/sec. Multi-threaded workloads, such as bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core tests, fall within the average tier. For machine learning tasks, the instance exhibits top-tier text generation speeds for small LLM models and strong performance for medium models, though prompt processing speeds remain average.

Given its compute-heavy configuration and lack of bundled local storage or GPUs, the c2d-highcpu-32 offers a cost-efficient profile for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly suitable for compute-intensive tasks such as image processing, software compilation, web serving, and moderate-scale text-based machine learning inference. It is less optimal for memory-intensive databases or workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 32 vCPUs (16 physical cores) and 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-memory ratio. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and encryption capabilities, alongside top-tier memory performance and small-model LLM text generation. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth generally align with mid-tier averages. This hardware configuration is highly suited for compute-bound applications, including software compilation, image processing, web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, delivering strong single-core CPU and memory performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring 32 vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, optimized for high-throughput CPU workloads and text generation.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores.
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a compute-focused 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio.
Top-tier memory performance and strong single-core CPU execution.
No bundled local storage or dedicated GPU accelerators.
Well-suited for software compilation, image processing, and CPU-based LLM inference.
